sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: SPACE caused a change! Looking at the image, the white block below the purple one now has a purple stripe at the top. It seems like SPACE is shifting colors or moving something within the white block. The white block (which was all white) now has a purple top portion. This looks like the purple block is "falling" or being moved into the white block's area. Let me check the segmentation to understand the exact change.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}

print("Before nodes:", len(before['nodes']), "After nodes:", len(after['nodes']))
print()

# Check for all changes
for nid in sorted(set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys()))):
    bn = before_nodes.get(nid)
    an = after_nodes.get(nid)
    if bn and an:
        if bn['hash'] != an['hash'] or bn['pixels'] != an['pixels'] or bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
            print(f"Node {nid} CHANGED:")
            print(f"  before: color={bn['color']}, pixels={bn['pixels']}, children={bn['children']}")
            print(f"  after:  color={an['color']}, pixels={an['pixels']}, children={an['children']}")
            if bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
                print(f"  boundary before: {bn['boundary']}")
                print(f"  boundary after:  {an['boundary']}")
    elif bn and not an:
        print(f"Node {nid} REMOVED: color={bn['color']}, pixels={bn['pixels']}")
    elif an and not bn:
        print(f"Node {nid} NEW: color={an['color']}, pixels={an['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Interesting! After pressing SPACE:
- Node 13 (was white, 100 pixels, 10x10 block at rows 34-43) changed to purple with only 50 pixels (now 5x10 at rows 34-38) - the top half became purple
- Node 14 (was charcoal bottom strip) changed to white with 50 pixels at rows 39-43 - the bottom half is now white
- A new node 15 (charcoal) appeared
So SPACE seems to be "splitting" or "shifting" the white block - the top half turned purple and the bottom half stayed white but moved. This looks like the purple color from the block above is "pouring" down into the white block.
The goal pattern (top-left) shows white on top, purple on bottom. Currently in the main area we have purple on top, and the white block is being filled from the top with purple.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe SPACE causes the top block to "pour" its color into the block below. If I keep pressing SPACE, maybe the entire white block will turn purple. But the goal is white on top, purple on bottom.
Wait - let me reconsider. Maybe I need to first select which block to pour, or maybe I need to use arrow keys to move things around. Let me try pressing SPACE again to see what happens, or try arrow keys.
